== name in kana (P1814) == * たなくらひこじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1130005009064 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '京都府京田辺市田辺棚倉49', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:棚倉孫神社]] {{wikidata link|Q54153264}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tanakurahiko Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Tanakurahiko Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kyōtanabe|Q816728|WD=Q816728|1=Kyōtanabe}} Tanabe Tanakura 49 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major shrine)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} 31 ({{ill|lt=623|Q31044|WD=Q31044|1=623}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} |神事 = |地図= {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Tanakurahiko Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tanakurahiko Shrine''' (たなくらひこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kyōtanabe|Q816728|WD=Q816728|1=Kyōtanabe}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha major shrine|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and its former shrine rank was gōsha. === History === === Outline === According to shrine tradition, it was invited in {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} 31 ({{ill|lt=623|Q31044|WD=Q31044|1=623}}) from Tanakura-no-shō in {{ill|lt=Sōraku District|Q1154661|WD=Q1154661|1=Sōraku District, Kyoto}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'', it was listed as a major shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called "Tenmangū." In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1931|Q18782|WD=Q18782|1=1931}}), in {{ill|lt=February|Q109|WD=Q109|1=February}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Divine rank === *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}: promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - listed as "Tanakurahiko-no-kami" === Cultural properties === *Main sanctuary ({{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}} {{ill|lt=Registered Tangible Cultural Property|Q11579194|WD=Q11579194|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan}}) Built in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Momoyama period|1=UNKNOWN}}. *Zuiki mikoshi ({{ill|lt=Kyōtanabe|Q816728|WD=Q816728|1=Kyōtanabe}} Intangible Cultural Property) For the annual festival, a zuiki mikoshi is made using taro stalks, chili peppers, kumquats, globe amaranth, white rice, beans, etc., and it parades through the ujiko district on {{ill|lt=October|Q124|WD=Q124|1=October}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Health and Sports Day|1=UNKNOWN}}. Tanakurahiko Jinja Honden.jpg|Main sanctuary Zuiki Mikoshi Tanakurahiko Jinja.jpg|Zuiki mikoshi === Access === *10 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kyōtanabe Station|Q855813|WD=Q855813|1=Kyōtanabe Station}} on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gakkentoshi Line|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} *15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Shin-Tanabe Station|Q7497135|WD=Q7497135|1=Shin-Tanabe Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kyoto Line|Q1057349|WD=Q1057349|lt=Kintetsu Kyoto Line|1=Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)}} of {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway|1=Kintetsu Railway}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *{{Wayback|url=http://www7b.biglobe.ne.jp/~tanakurahiko/ |title=棚倉孫神社 |date=20090225193248}}- (official site) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tanakurahiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tanakurahiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京田辺市の建築物]] [[Category:京田辺市の歴史]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]][[Category:qq]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * たなくらひこじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1130005009064 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '京都府京田辺市田辺棚倉49', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:棚倉孫神社]] {{wikidata link|Q54153264}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tanakurahiko Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Tanakurahiko Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kyōtanabe|Q816728|WD=Q816728|1=Kyōtanabe}} Tanabe Tanakura 49 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major shrine)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} 31 ({{ill|lt=623|Q31044|WD=Q31044|1=623}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} |神事 = |地図= {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Tanakurahiko Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tanakurahiko Shrine''' (たなくらひこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kyōtanabe|Q816728|WD=Q816728|1=Kyōtanabe}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha major shrine|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and its former shrine rank was gōsha. === History === === Outline === According to shrine tradition, it was invited in {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} 31 ({{ill|lt=623|Q31044|WD=Q31044|1=623}}) from Tanakura-no-shō in {{ill|lt=Sōraku District|Q1154661|WD=Q1154661|1=Sōraku District, Kyoto}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'', it was listed as a major shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called "Tenmangū." In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1931|Q18782|WD=Q18782|1=1931}}), in {{ill|lt=February|Q109|WD=Q109|1=February}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Divine rank === *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}: promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - listed as "Tanakurahiko-no-kami" === Cultural properties === *Main sanctuary ({{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}} {{ill|lt=Registered Tangible Cultural Property|Q11579194|WD=Q11579194|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan}}) Built in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Momoyama period|1=UNKNOWN}}. *Zuiki mikoshi ({{ill|lt=Kyōtanabe|Q816728|WD=Q816728|1=Kyōtanabe}} Intangible Cultural Property) For the annual festival, a zuiki mikoshi is made using taro stalks, chili peppers, kumquats, globe amaranth, white rice, beans, etc., and it parades through the ujiko district on {{ill|lt=October|Q124|WD=Q124|1=October}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Health and Sports Day|1=UNKNOWN}}. Tanakurahiko Jinja Honden.jpg|Main sanctuary Zuiki Mikoshi Tanakurahiko Jinja.jpg|Zuiki mikoshi === Access === *10 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kyōtanabe Station|Q855813|WD=Q855813|1=Kyōtanabe Station}} on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gakkentoshi Line|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} *15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Shin-Tanabe Station|Q7497135|WD=Q7497135|1=Shin-Tanabe Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kyoto Line|Q1057349|WD=Q1057349|lt=Kintetsu Kyoto Line|1=Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)}} of {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway|1=Kintetsu Railway}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *{{Wayback|url=http://www7b.biglobe.ne.jp/~tanakurahiko/ |title=棚倉孫神社 |date=20090225193248}}- (official site) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tanakurahiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tanakurahiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京田辺市の建築物]] [[Category:京田辺市の歴史]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * たなくらひこじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1130005009064 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '京都府京田辺市田辺棚倉49', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:棚倉孫神社]] {{wikidata link|Q54153264}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tanakurahiko Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Tanakurahiko Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kyōtanabe|Q816728|WD=Q816728|1=Kyōtanabe}} Tanabe Tanakura 49 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major shrine)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} 31 ({{ill|lt=623|Q31044|WD=Q31044|1=623}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} |神事 = |地図= {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Tanakurahiko Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tanakurahiko Shrine''' (たなくらひこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kyōtanabe|Q816728|WD=Q816728|1=Kyōtanabe}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha major shrine|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and its former shrine rank was gōsha. === History === === Outline === According to shrine tradition, it was invited in {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} 31 ({{ill|lt=623|Q31044|WD=Q31044|1=623}}) from Tanakura-no-shō in {{ill|lt=Sōraku District|Q1154661|WD=Q1154661|1=Sōraku District, Kyoto}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'', it was listed as a major shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called "Tenmangū." In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1931|Q18782|WD=Q18782|1=1931}}), in {{ill|lt=February|Q109|WD=Q109|1=February}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Divine rank === *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}: promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - listed as "Tanakurahiko-no-kami" === Cultural properties === *Main sanctuary ({{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}} {{ill|lt=Registered Tangible Cultural Property|Q11579194|WD=Q11579194|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan}}) Built in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Momoyama period|1=UNKNOWN}}. *Zuiki mikoshi ({{ill|lt=Kyōtanabe|Q816728|WD=Q816728|1=Kyōtanabe}} Intangible Cultural Property) For the annual festival, a zuiki mikoshi is made using taro stalks, chili peppers, kumquats, globe amaranth, white rice, beans, etc., and it parades through the ujiko district on {{ill|lt=October|Q124|WD=Q124|1=October}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Health and Sports Day|1=UNKNOWN}}. Tanakurahiko Jinja Honden.jpg|Main sanctuary Zuiki Mikoshi Tanakurahiko Jinja.jpg|Zuiki mikoshi === Access === *10 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kyōtanabe Station|Q855813|WD=Q855813|1=Kyōtanabe Station}} on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gakkentoshi Line|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} *15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Shin-Tanabe Station|Q7497135|WD=Q7497135|1=Shin-Tanabe Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kyoto Line|Q1057349|WD=Q1057349|lt=Kintetsu Kyoto Line|1=Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)}} of {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway|1=Kintetsu Railway}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *{{Wayback|url=http://www7b.biglobe.ne.jp/~tanakurahiko/ |title=棚倉孫神社 |date=20090225193248}}- (official site) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tanakurahiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tanakurahiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:京田辺市の建築物]] [[Category:京田辺市の歴史]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * たなくらひこじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1130005009064 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '京都府京田辺市田辺棚倉49', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:棚倉孫神社]] {{wikidata link|Q54153264}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tanakurahiko Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Tanakurahiko Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kyōtanabe|Q816728|WD=Q816728|1=Kyōtanabe}} Tanabe Tanakura 49 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major shrine)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} 31 ({{ill|lt=623|Q31044|WD=Q31044|1=623}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} |神事 = |地図= {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Tanakurahiko Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tanakurahiko Shrine''' (たなくらひこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kyōtanabe|Q816728|WD=Q816728|1=Kyōtanabe}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha major shrine|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and its former shrine rank was gōsha. === History === === Outline === According to shrine tradition, it was invited in {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} 31 ({{ill|lt=623|Q31044|WD=Q31044|1=623}}) from Tanakura-no-shō in {{ill|lt=Sōraku District|Q1154661|WD=Q1154661|1=Sōraku District, Kyoto}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'', it was listed as a major shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called "Tenmangū." In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1931|Q18782|WD=Q18782|1=1931}}), in {{ill|lt=February|Q109|WD=Q109|1=February}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Divine rank === *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}: promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - listed as "Tanakurahiko-no-kami" === Cultural properties === *Main sanctuary ({{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}} {{ill|lt=Registered Tangible Cultural Property|Q11579194|WD=Q11579194|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan}}) Built in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Momoyama period|1=UNKNOWN}}. *Zuiki mikoshi ({{ill|lt=Kyōtanabe|Q816728|WD=Q816728|1=Kyōtanabe}} Intangible Cultural Property) For the annual festival, a zuiki mikoshi is made using taro stalks, chili peppers, kumquats, globe amaranth, white rice, beans, etc., and it parades through the ujiko district on {{ill|lt=October|Q124|WD=Q124|1=October}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Health and Sports Day|1=UNKNOWN}}. Tanakurahiko Jinja Honden.jpg|Main sanctuary Zuiki Mikoshi Tanakurahiko Jinja.jpg|Zuiki mikoshi === Access === *10 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kyōtanabe Station|Q855813|WD=Q855813|1=Kyōtanabe Station}} on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gakkentoshi Line|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} *15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Shin-Tanabe Station|Q7497135|WD=Q7497135|1=Shin-Tanabe Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kyoto Line|Q1057349|WD=Q1057349|lt=Kintetsu Kyoto Line|1=Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)}} of {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway|1=Kintetsu Railway}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *{{Wayback|url=http://www7b.biglobe.ne.jp/~tanakurahiko/ |title=棚倉孫神社 |date=20090225193248}}- (official site) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tanakurahiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tanakurahiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:京田辺市の建築物]] [[Category:History of Kyotanabe, Kyoto]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * たなくらひこじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1130005009064 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '京都府京田辺市田辺棚倉49', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:棚倉孫神社]] {{wikidata link|Q54153264}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tanakurahiko Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Tanakurahiko Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kyōtanabe|Q816728|WD=Q816728|1=Kyōtanabe}} Tanabe Tanakura 49 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major shrine)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} 31 ({{ill|lt=623|Q31044|WD=Q31044|1=623}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} |神事 = |地図= {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Tanakurahiko Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tanakurahiko Shrine''' (たなくらひこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kyōtanabe|Q816728|WD=Q816728|1=Kyōtanabe}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha major shrine|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and its former shrine rank was gōsha. === History === === Outline === According to shrine tradition, it was invited in {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} 31 ({{ill|lt=623|Q31044|WD=Q31044|1=623}}) from Tanakura-no-shō in {{ill|lt=Sōraku District|Q1154661|WD=Q1154661|1=Sōraku District, Kyoto}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'', it was listed as a major shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called "Tenmangū." In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1931|Q18782|WD=Q18782|1=1931}}), in {{ill|lt=February|Q109|WD=Q109|1=February}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Divine rank === *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}: promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - listed as "Tanakurahiko-no-kami" === Cultural properties === *Main sanctuary ({{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}} {{ill|lt=Registered Tangible Cultural Property|Q11579194|WD=Q11579194|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan}}) Built in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Momoyama period|1=UNKNOWN}}. *Zuiki mikoshi ({{ill|lt=Kyōtanabe|Q816728|WD=Q816728|1=Kyōtanabe}} Intangible Cultural Property) For the annual festival, a zuiki mikoshi is made using taro stalks, chili peppers, kumquats, globe amaranth, white rice, beans, etc., and it parades through the ujiko district on {{ill|lt=October|Q124|WD=Q124|1=October}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Health and Sports Day|1=UNKNOWN}}. Tanakurahiko Jinja Honden.jpg|Main sanctuary Zuiki Mikoshi Tanakurahiko Jinja.jpg|Zuiki mikoshi === Access === *10 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kyōtanabe Station|Q855813|WD=Q855813|1=Kyōtanabe Station}} on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gakkentoshi Line|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} *15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Shin-Tanabe Station|Q7497135|WD=Q7497135|1=Shin-Tanabe Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kyoto Line|Q1057349|WD=Q1057349|lt=Kintetsu Kyoto Line|1=Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)}} of {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway|1=Kintetsu Railway}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *{{Wayback|url=http://www7b.biglobe.ne.jp/~tanakurahiko/ |title=棚倉孫神社 |date=20090225193248}}- (official site) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tanakurahiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tanakurahiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:京田辺市の建築物]] [[Category:History of Kyotanabe, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * たなくらひこじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1130005009064 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '京都府京田辺市田辺棚倉49', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:棚倉孫神社]] {{wikidata link|Q54153264}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tanakurahiko Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Tanakurahiko Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kyōtanabe|Q816728|WD=Q816728|1=Kyōtanabe}} Tanabe Tanakura 49 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major shrine)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} 31 ({{ill|lt=623|Q31044|WD=Q31044|1=623}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} |神事 = |地図= {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Tanakurahiko Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tanakurahiko Shrine''' (たなくらひこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kyōtanabe|Q816728|WD=Q816728|1=Kyōtanabe}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha major shrine|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and its former shrine rank was gōsha. === History === === Outline === According to shrine tradition, it was invited in {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} 31 ({{ill|lt=623|Q31044|WD=Q31044|1=623}}) from Tanakura-no-shō in {{ill|lt=Sōraku District|Q1154661|WD=Q1154661|1=Sōraku District, Kyoto}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'', it was listed as a major shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called "Tenmangū." In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1931|Q18782|WD=Q18782|1=1931}}), in {{ill|lt=February|Q109|WD=Q109|1=February}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Divine rank === *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}: promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - listed as "Tanakurahiko-no-kami" === Cultural properties === *Main sanctuary ({{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}} {{ill|lt=Registered Tangible Cultural Property|Q11579194|WD=Q11579194|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan}}) Built in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Momoyama period|1=UNKNOWN}}. *Zuiki mikoshi ({{ill|lt=Kyōtanabe|Q816728|WD=Q816728|1=Kyōtanabe}} Intangible Cultural Property) For the annual festival, a zuiki mikoshi is made using taro stalks, chili peppers, kumquats, globe amaranth, white rice, beans, etc., and it parades through the ujiko district on {{ill|lt=October|Q124|WD=Q124|1=October}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Health and Sports Day|1=UNKNOWN}}. Tanakurahiko Jinja Honden.jpg|Main sanctuary Zuiki Mikoshi Tanakurahiko Jinja.jpg|Zuiki mikoshi === Access === *10 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kyōtanabe Station|Q855813|WD=Q855813|1=Kyōtanabe Station}} on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gakkentoshi Line|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} *15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Shin-Tanabe Station|Q7497135|WD=Q7497135|1=Shin-Tanabe Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kyoto Line|Q1057349|WD=Q1057349|lt=Kintetsu Kyoto Line|1=Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)}} of {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway|1=Kintetsu Railway}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *{{Wayback|url=http://www7b.biglobe.ne.jp/~tanakurahiko/ |title=棚倉孫神社 |date=20090225193248}}- (official site) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tanakurahiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tanakurahiko Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:京田辺市の建築物]] [[Category:History of Kyotanabe, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * たなくらひこじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1130005009064 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '京都府京田辺市田辺棚倉49', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:棚倉孫神社]] {{wikidata link|Q54153264}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tanakurahiko Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Tanakurahiko Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kyōtanabe|Q816728|WD=Q816728|1=Kyōtanabe}} Tanabe Tanakura 49 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Amenokaguyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Kaguyama}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major shrine)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} 31 ({{ill|lt=623|Q31044|WD=Q31044|1=623}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} |神事 = |地図= {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Tanakurahiko Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tanakurahiko Shrine''' (たなくらひこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kyōtanabe|Q816728|WD=Q816728|1=Kyōtanabe}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha major shrine|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and its former shrine rank was gōsha. === History === === Outline === According to shrine tradition, it was invited in {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} 31 ({{ill|lt=623|Q31044|WD=Q31044|1=623}}) from Tanakura-no-shō in {{ill|lt=Sōraku District|Q1154661|WD=Q1154661|1=Sōraku District, Kyoto}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'', it was listed as a major shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called "Tenmangū." In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1931|Q18782|WD=Q18782|1=1931}}), in {{ill|lt=February|Q109|WD=Q109|1=February}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Divine rank === *{{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}: promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - listed as "Tanakurahiko-no-kami" === Cultural properties === *Main sanctuary ({{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}} {{ill|lt=Registered Tangible Cultural Property|Q11579194|WD=Q11579194|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan}}) Built in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Momoyama period|1=UNKNOWN}}. *Zuiki mikoshi ({{ill|lt=Kyōtanabe|Q816728|WD=Q816728|1=Kyōtanabe}} Intangible Cultural Property) For the annual festival, a zuiki mikoshi is made using taro stalks, chili peppers, kumquats, globe amaranth, white rice, beans, etc., and it parades through the ujiko district on {{ill|lt=October|Q124|WD=Q124|1=October}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Health and Sports Day|1=UNKNOWN}}. Tanakurahiko Jinja Honden.jpg|Main sanctuary Zuiki Mikoshi Tanakurahiko Jinja.jpg|Zuiki mikoshi === Access === *10 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kyōtanabe Station|Q855813|WD=Q855813|1=Kyōtanabe Station}} on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gakkentoshi Line|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} *15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Shin-Tanabe Station|Q7497135|WD=Q7497135|1=Shin-Tanabe Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kyoto Line|Q1057349|WD=Q1057349|lt=Kintetsu Kyoto Line|1=Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)}} of {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway|1=Kintetsu Railway}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *{{Wayback|url=http://www7b.biglobe.ne.jp/~tanakurahiko/ |title=棚倉孫神社 |date=20090225193248}}- (official site) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tanakurahiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tanakurahiko Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:京田辺市の建築物]] [[Category:History of Kyotanabe, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]]
